steps.title: Identify the punctuation rule
steps.content: The sentence opens a parenthetical with an em dash after postmodern dance. In Standard English, a parenthetical opened by an em dash must be closed by a matching em dash before the main clause resumes. The blank falls at the end of the parenthetical list and must supply that closing em dash.

steps.title: Test each choice for correct em-dash pairing
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘gravity),’: closes the parenthesis but follows it with a comma — a comma cannot substitute for the closing em dash, leaving the parenthetical bracket mismatched. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘gravity);’: closes the parenthesis but follows it with a semicolon — a semicolon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical and would require an independent clause after it, which “to video” is not. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘gravity)—’: closes the parenthesis and supplies the required em dash — the paired em dashes correctly bracket the entire parenthetical clause, allowing the sentence to continue with “to video.” ✓
  • Choice D — ‘gravity)’: closes the parenthesis but supplies no closing punctuation for the em-dash parenthetical — the opening em dash is left unmatched and “to video” floats without proper attachment. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
